title: Treatment outcomes for oropharyngeal cancer: findings from an institutional study.
abstract: Locally advanced oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma (OPSCC) represents a growing clinical challenge, primarily due to the increasing incidence of human papillomavirus (HPV)-associated disease. While treatment options include radiotherapy (RT) alone, RT with Cetuximab (RCX), or radiochemotherapy (RCT), the latter remains the standard of care. This retrospective study analyzed 359 patients with locally advanced OPSCC treated at CIUSSS de l'Estrie-CHUS since 2011 to evaluate how HPV status influences outcomes across different treatment regimens.
